subject: [PATCH v4 0/4] mm: improve write performance with RWF_DONTCACHE

2026-05-01.patch_summary: This patch series aims to improve write performance when using the RWF_DONTCACHE flag by implementing a scheme that kicks the flusher thread to do an amount of writeback proportional to the amount written after a DONTCACHE write. The approach was suggested by Jan Kara and Christoph Hellwig during review of earlier versions. The patches track dirty pages per bdi_writeback, kick the flusher thread instead of initiating writeback inline, and add mechanisms for running 'perf lock' around test cases. Benchmark results show marked improvement in RWF_DONTCACHE write performance across various tests.

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where a DONTCACHE write is followed by an fsync, which can cause data corruption.

Jan Kara pointed out that the patch description inaccurately states that only dirty dontcache pages are written back, when in fact arbitrary dirty pages are written back, but the amount is influenced by WB_DONTCACHE_DIRTY. He suggested rephrasing the sentence to clarify this and noted that this heuristic improves performance over other approaches.
reviews.raw_body:
Nice and looks good to me now. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

One nit below:

---

This comment is a bit confusing as in fact we write arbitrary dirty pages.
It is only the amount of pages that is influenced by WB_DONTCACHE_DIRTY. So
I'd rephrase the last sentence like: We queue writeback for the inode's wb
for as many pages as there are dontcache pages but we don't restrict
writeback to dontcache pages only. This significantly improves performance
over either writing all wb's pages or writing only dontcache pages.
Although it doesn't guarantee quick writeback and reclaim of dontcache
pages it keeps the amount of dirty pages in check and over longer term
dontcache pages get written and reclaimed by background writeback even with
this rough heuristic.

								Honza

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch series does not include a mechanism to handle concurrent DONTCACHE writes, which could lead to writeback issues and requested the addition of such a mechanism.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks good. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza

The approach was suggested by Jan Kara and Christoph Hellwig during review of earlier versions. The patches track dirty pages per bdi_writeback, kick the flusher thread instead of initiating writeback inline, and add mechanisms for running 'perf lock' around test cases. Benchmark results show marked improvement in RWF_DONTCACHE write performance across various tests.
